overview.description: Collaborative data science notebook and analytics workspace where AI agents and humans work together
overview.description_long: Deepnote is a collaborative data science and analytics platform built around Jupyter-compatible notebooks. It enables data teams to explore, analyze, and interpret data together in a shared workspace. The platform features AI-powered automation that can query, analyze, and interpret data without coding skills. Deepnote understands business context and data to deliver high-quality analyses, positioning itself as a data workspace where agents and humans collaborate.

history.origin_story: Founded in 2019 in Prague by Jakub Jurovych and co-founders, Deepnote set out to build a better data science notebook. The team went through Y Combinator and raised seed funding from Index Ventures in early 2020, followed by a $20M Series A in 2022 led by Accel and Index Ventures.

key_milestones.event: Raised $3.8M seed round from Index Ventures (Feb 2020)
Raised $20M Series A led by Index Ventures and Accel (Jan 2022)
Named in Top 10 Largest Funding Rounds in Czech Republic 2022
Launched new notebook format and AI-powered data workspace features

awards: Top 10 Largest Czech Funding Rounds 2022 (The Recursive)

metadata.notes: Deepnote homepage (deepnote.com) blocked direct browser navigation. Glassdoor search could not be completed due to browser session conflicts. Series A company with $23.8M total funding. TrueUp reports ~40 employees and 6 open roles. LinkedIn shows 12.3K+ followers. Golden.com also lists San Francisco as a location, suggesting dual Prague/SF presence. Revenue data not publicly available.
